name: fastui
documentation:
# FastUI

[![CI](https://github.com/pydantic/FastUI/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/pydantic/FastUI/actions?query=event%3Apush+branch%3Amain+workflow%3ACI)
[![versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/fastui.svg)](https://github.com/pydantic/FastUI)
[![license](https://img.shields.io/github/license/pydantic/FastUI.svg)](https://github.com/pydantic/FastUI/blob/main/LICENSE)

Python components for [FastUI](https://github.com/pydantic/FastUI).

classes.name: fastui.components.display.DisplayMode

class_variables.name: fastui.components.display.DisplayMode.auto
documentation: default, same as None below
Display mode for a value.

classes.name: fastui.components.display.DisplayBase

class_variables.name: fastui.components.display.DisplayBase.mode
documentation: Display mode for the value.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.display.DisplayBase.title
documentation: Title to display for the value.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.display.DisplayBase.on_click
documentation: Event to trigger when the value is clicked.
Base class for display components.

classes.name: fastui.components.display.DisplayLookup

class_variables.name: fastui.components.display.DisplayLookup.field
documentation: Field to display.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.display.DisplayLookup.table_width_percent
documentation: Percentage width - 0 to 100, specific to tables.
Description of how to display a value looked up from data, either in a table or detail view.

classes.name: fastui.components.display.Display

class_variables.name: fastui.components.display.Display.value
documentation: Value to display.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.display.Display.type
documentation: The type of the component. Always 'Display'.
Description of how to display a value, either in a table or detail view.

classes.name: fastui.components.display.Details

class_variables.name: fastui.components.display.Details.class_name
documentation: Optional class name to apply to the details component.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.display.Details.type
documentation: The type of the component. Always 'Details'.
Details associated with displaying a data model.

variables.name: fastui.components.forms.FormField
documentation: Union of all form field types.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.forms.BaseFormField.locked

class_variables.name: fastui.components.forms.BaseFormField.class_name
documentation: Optional class name to apply to the field's HTML component.

classes.name: fastui.components.forms.BaseForm

documentation: URL to submit the form data to.

documentation: Initial values for the form fields, mapping field names to values.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.forms.BaseForm.method
documentation: HTTP method to use for the form submission.

documentation: Display mode for the form.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.forms.BaseForm.submit_on_change
documentation: Whether to submit the form on change.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.forms.BaseForm.submit_trigger
documentation: Event to trigger form submission.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.forms.BaseForm.loading
documentation: Components to display while the form is submitting.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.forms.BaseForm.footer
documentation: Components to display in the form footer.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.forms.BaseForm.class_name
documentation: Optional class name to apply to the form's HTML component.

classes.name: fastui.components.forms.Form

class_variables.name: fastui.components.forms.Form.type
documentation: The type of the component. Always 'Form'.

classes.name: fastui.components.tables.Table

class_variables.name: fastui.components.tables.Table.data
documentation: Sequence of data models to display in the table.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.tables.Table.columns
documentation: List of columns to display in the table. If not provided, columns will be inferred from the data model.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.tables.Table.data_model
documentation: Data model to use for the table. If not provided, the model will be inferred from the first data item.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.tables.Table.no_data_message
documentation: Message to display when there is no data.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.tables.Table.class_name
documentation: Optional class name to apply to the paragraph's HTML component.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.tables.Table.type
documentation: The type of the component. Always 'Table'.
Table component.
TODO allow dataclasses and typed dicts here too

classes.name: fastui.components.tables.Pagination

class_variables.name: fastui.components.tables.Pagination.page
documentation: The current page number.

functions.name: fastui.auth.shared.fastapi_auth_exception_handling
documentation: Register an exception handler for any `AuthException` in a FastAPI app.

classes.name: fastui.auth.shared.AuthException
documentation: Base exception for all auth-related errors.

classes.name: fastui.auth.shared.AuthRedirect
documentation:
Special exception which should cause a 345 HTTP response with a body containing
FastUI components to redirect the user to a new page.

modules.name: fastui.components
documentation:
Component definitions.

All CamelCase names in the namespace should be components.

variables.name: fastui.components.CodeStyle
documentation:
Code style to apply to a `Code` component.

Attributes:
    codeStyle: The code style to apply. If None, no style is applied.

See Also:
    - [PrismJS Themes](https://github.com/PrismJS/prism-themes)
    - [PrismJS Theme Index](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/react-syntax-highlighter@15.5.0/dist/esm/styles/prism/index.js)

variables.name: fastui.components.AnyComponent
documentation:
Union of all components.

Pydantic discriminator field is set to 'type' to allow for efficient serialization and deserialization of the components.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.Text.text
documentation: The text to display.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.Text.type
documentation: The type of the component. Always 'Text'.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.Paragraph.class_name
documentation: Optional class name to apply to the paragraph's HTML component.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.Paragraph.type
documentation: The type of the component. Always 'Paragraph'.

classes.name: fastui.components.Div

class_variables.name: fastui.components.Div.components

class_variables.name: fastui.components.Div.class_name
documentation: Optional class name to apply to the div's HTML component.

class_variables.name: fastui.components.Div.type

classes.name: fastui.components.Page

class_variables.name: fastui.components.Page.components

Similar to `container` in many UI frameworks, this acts as a root component for most pages.

functions.name: fastui.prebuilt_html
documentation:
Returns a simple HTML page which includes the FastUI react frontend, loaded from https://www.jsdelivr.com/.

Arguments:
    title: page title
    api_root_url: the root URL of the API backend, which will be used to get data, default is '/api'.
    api_path_mode: whether to append the page path to the root API request URL, or use it as a query parameter,
        default is 'append'.
    api_path_strip: string to remove from the start of the page path before making the API request.

Returns:
    HTML string which can be returned by an endpoint to serve the FastUI frontend.

classes.name: fastui.FastUI
documentation: The root component of a FastUI application.

classes.name: fastui.auth.github.GitHubAuthProvider

methods.name: fastui.auth.github.GitHubAuthProvider.__init__
documentation:
Arguments:
    httpx_client: An instance of `httpx.AsyncClient` to use for making requests to GitHub.
    github_client_id: The client ID of the GitHub OAuth app.
    github_client_secret: The client secret of the GitHub OAuth app.
    redirect_uri: The URL in your app where users will be sent after authorization, if custom
    scopes: See https://docs.github.com/en/apps/oauth-apps/building-oauth-apps/scopes-for-oauth-apps#available-scopes
    state_provider: If `True`, use a `StateProvider` to generate and validate state parameters for the OAuth
        flow, you can also provide an instance directly.
    exchange_cache_age: If not `None`,
        responses from the access token exchange are cached for the given duration.

methods.name: fastui.auth.github.GitHubAuthProvider.create
documentation: Async context manager to create a GitHubAuth instance with a new `httpx.AsyncClient`.

methods.name: fastui.auth.github.GitHubAuthProvider.authorization_url
documentation: See https://docs.github.com/en/apps/oauth-apps/building-oauth-apps/authorizing-oauth-apps#1-request-a-users-github-identity

methods.name: fastui.auth.github.GitHubAuthProvider.exchange_code
documentation:
Exchange a code for an access token.

If `self._exchange_cache_age` is not `None` (the default), responses are cached for the given duration to
work around issues with React often sending the same request multiple times in development mode.

methods.name: fastui.auth.github.GitHubAuthProvider.get_github_user
documentation: See https://docs.github.com/en/rest/users/users#get-the-authenticated-user

methods.name: fastui.auth.github.GitHubAuthProvider.get_github_user_emails
documentation: See https://docs.github.com/en/rest/users/emails
For details see https://docs.github.com/en/apps/oauth-apps/building-oauth-apps/authorizing-oauth-apps.

methods.name: fastui.auth.github.ExchangeCache._purge
documentation: Remove old items from the exchange cache

classes.name: fastui.auth.github.StateProvider
documentation:
This is a simple state provider for the GitHub OAuth flow which uses a JWT to create an unguessable "state" string.

Requires `PyJWT` to be installed.

classes.name: fastui.types.JsonDataSchema
documentation: TODO: replace with https://docs.pydantic.dev/dev/api/types/#pydantic.types.JsonValue, maybe?

functions.name: fastui.forms.unflatten
documentation:
Unflatten a `FormData` dict into a nested dict.

Also omit empty strings, this might be a bit controversial, but it helps in many scenarios, e.g. a select
which hasn't been updated. It also avoids empty values for string inputs that haven't been fill in.

functions.name: fastui.forms.Textarea
documentation: Use uppercase for consistency with pydantic.Field, which is also a function

classes.name: fastui.forms.FastUIForm
documentation: TODO mypy, pyright and pycharm don't understand the model type if this is used, is there a way to get it to work?

methods.name: fastui.forms.FormFile._validate_file
documentation:
See https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input/file#unique_file_type_specifiers
for details on what's allowed.

modules.name: fastui.class_name
documentation: could be renamed to something general if there's more to add

functions.name: fastui.json_schema.loc_to_name
documentation:
Convert a loc to a string if any item contains a '.' or the first item starts with '[' then encode with JSON,
otherwise join with '.'.

The sister method `name_to_loc` is in `form_extra.py`.

functions.name: fastui.json_schema.deference_json_schema
documentation: Convert a schema which might be a reference or union to a concrete schema.

functions.name: fastui.json_schema.input_html_type
documentation: Convert a schema into an HTML type

functions.name: fastui.json_schema.schema_is_field
documentation: Determine if a schema is a field `JsonSchemaField`

functions.name: fastui.json_schema.schema_is_array

functions.name: fastui.json_schema.schema_is_object
